claim_scope: H/M development-only full-cell count-matched training preregistration; current admitted view is not training-submittable; not product accuracy, hardware validation, shadow/final validation, full ten-material matrix, or manuscript-grade powder XRD

primary_count_balance_strategy.reason: Preregistered main target for the next source-side matrix. The current admitted view does not have enough matched-pair support at this window.

strict_sensitivity_strategy.reason: Strict sensitivity window for the next source-side matrix. This is not a weaker fallback; it is a stricter preregistered sensitivity check.

wide_window_diagnostic_reference.interpretation: Wide windows can reach support on the current view, but they did not produce admissible training evidence because total-count-only and shuffled-label controls remained too strong.

source_side_matrix_preregistration.intent: Increase source-side pair replicates before any new training so the primary 0.020 and strict 0.015 count-matched windows can plausibly exceed the 300/200/200 matched-pair target without widening to the confounded 0.040/0.050 windows.

next_allowed_actions: run this prereg audit to machine-confirm the current full-cell view is not training-submittable at 0.020 or 0.015
generate the source-side count-matched full-cell matrix from the preregistered 5-replicate config only after the prereg gate passes
run matrix preflight before any Geant4 execution
run development-only Geant4 and feature extraction only if the matrix preflight passes
rerun final data audit and the full-cell ordinary/count-balanced training gate only after new features exist
stop_loss_actions: if 0.020 support is still below 300/200/200 after the new matrix, increase source-side support or redesign count targets before training
if 0.015 strict support is below 300/200/200, report strict sensitivity as underpowered and do not use it for promotion
if total-count-only remains >= 0.60, return to source/count normalization design
if shuffled-label null p95 remains >= 0.55, return to null protocol and feature representation review
never promote by using advanced models to bypass failed controls
